title: Dry eye
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped redness, pain, photophobia, discharge, and contact-lens red-eye history as symptom or triage context.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped trauma, chemical, vision, severe-pain, photophobia, contact-lens, corneal, and severe-redness warning fields as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

investigations.text: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped dry-eye identity, grittiness, visual fluctuation, and clinician-performed ocular-surface staining context without symptom-only diagnosis.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports dry-eye symptom screening, visual symptoms, tear-film tests, fluorescein or lissamine staining, and cornea, conjunctiva, or eyelid-margin observation without inferring diagnosis from symptoms alone.

source.title: TFOS DEWS II Diagnostic Methodology report

exact_section.heading: Clinical protocol for the dry-eye diagnostic test battery

investigations.text: Supports screen-related visual discomfort, pain or photophobia as differential context, and contact-lens-associated dry-eye context without treatment instructions.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens and screen-related differential context; treatment is not mapped.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports screen-related visual discomfort, pain or photophobia as differential context, and contact-lens-associated dry-eye context without treatment instructions.

source.title: TFOS DEWS II Diagnostic Methodology report

exact_section.heading: Differential diagnosis, visual asthenopia, and contact lenses

assessment.text: Supports clinician-observed corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein-staining documentation in dry-eye evaluation.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein fields only when assessed.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-observed corneal, conjunctival, eyelid, and fluorescein-staining documentation in dry-eye evaluation.

source.title: TFOS DEWS II Diagnostic Methodology report

exact_section.heading: Ocular-surface damage and eyelid assessment
exact_section.locator: Sections 6.6 and 6.8; official report lines 327–365 and 402–463

referral.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens-care, return, worsening, vision-change, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation without doses.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped visual-acuity and pupil findings only when assessed.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source_limitations: Lens-hygiene and wear-duration history, symptom duration, external eye, eye movements, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, lens-fit investigation, generic management, patient questions, medication plan, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Dry-eye diagnosis, subtype, contact-lens complication, infection, trauma, chemical injury, or corneal disease must not be inferred from symptoms alone.
Treatment, medicine choice or dose, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
